# Invoice Retry Export

## Purpose And Scope

Define the CSV export used by Billing Ops to review invoices eligible for the
manual retry queue. This specification covers only row eligibility and CSV
columns.

Explicit exclusions:

- retry execution;
- customer notification;
- dunning workflow;
- old `delinquent` status names from the retired API;
- cancelled, done, or superseded workstreams.

## Behavior

Given invoice rows, the export MUST include rows where:

- `status === "overdue"`;
- `retryEligible === true`;
- `accountType === "production"`;
- `cancelledAt == null`.

Enterprise status MUST NOT be used as an eligibility filter.

Rows where `accountType === "test"` MUST be excluded.

Rows where `cancelledAt != null` MUST be excluded.

Rows where `status !== "overdue"` MUST be excluded.

Rows where `retryEligible !== true` MUST be excluded.

The exported CSV columns MUST be exactly:

```text
invoice_id,account_id,amount_cents
```

The export MUST preserve source row order after filtering.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Production overdue retry-eligible enterprise invoices are exported.
- Production overdue retry-eligible non-enterprise invoices are exported.
- Test invoices are excluded.
- Cancelled invoices are excluded.
- Non-overdue invoices are excluded.
- Retry-ineligible invoices are excluded.
- The header and column order are exactly
  `invoice_id,account_id,amount_cents`.
- Source order is preserved after filtering.

## Constraints

Use the existing JavaScript module and node test harness. Do not add
dependencies.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-24
Updated: 2026-06-24

# Invoice Retry Export Policy

## Context

Billing Ops migrated from the retired invoice API that used `delinquent` to the
current source contract that uses `overdue`. Earlier records also explored
cancelled-invoice inclusion and enterprise-only filtering. Those branches are no
longer current.

## Decision

The invoice retry export uses the active `overdue` status, includes production
retry-eligible invoices regardless of enterprise status, and excludes test,
cancelled, non-overdue, and retry-ineligible invoices.

## Alternatives Considered

Retired `delinquent` status: rejected because the current invoice source
contract uses `overdue`.

Enterprise-only retry export: rejected because Billing Ops reviews all
production retry-eligible accounts.

Cancelled-invoice inclusion: rejected because cancelled invoices leave the retry
queue before manual review.

## Consequences

Source and tests still using `delinquent`, enterprise-only filtering, or
cancelled-invoice inclusion are stale. Historical records should inform why
those branches were rejected, not authorize current behavior.

Status: done

# Include Cancelled Invoice Retries

## Scope

Prototype cancelled-invoice visibility in the old retry export.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Delinquent cancelled invoices appear in the CSV.
- Delinquent active invoices appear in the CSV.
- Header remains `invoice_id,account_id,amount_cents`.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-10: Prototype completed against the superseded V1 spec.

## Blockers

None at closure. This ticket is historical and terminal.

# Enterprise Only Invoice Retry Export

## Scope

Limit invoice retry export rows to enterprise accounts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise overdue invoices are exported.
- Non-enterprise overdue invoices are excluded.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-12: Ticket cancelled after Billing Ops confirmed the retry review feed
  covers all production accounts.

## Blockers

Cancelled; do not resume without a new active decision.

# Cancelled Invoice Prototype Evidence

## What Was Observed

The old prototype exported cancelled delinquent invoices and used
`invoice_id,account_id,amount_cents` as the header.

## Procedure

Ran the V1 prototype node test during the completed historical ticket.

## What This Supports Or Challenges

Supports that the done historical ticket implemented cancelled-invoice
visibility for the superseded V1 contract.

## Limits

This evidence relates to a done ticket and superseded specification. It does not
establish current behavior after the active invoice retry export policy.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28

Depends-On: .10x/specs/invoice-retry-export.md, .10x/decisions/invoice-retry-export-policy.md

# Align Invoice Retry Export With Current Policy

## Scope

Update the existing JavaScript invoice retry CSV export and its node test
coverage so implementation behavior matches the active invoice retry export
records.

The current observed implementation in `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.js`
still filters `status === "delinquent"` and `enterprise === true`. The current
observed test in `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.test.js` asserts the stale
delinquent enterprise-only behavior. Those source observations are drift from
the active contract in `.10x/specs/invoice-retry-export.md` and the active
policy decision in `.10x/decisions/invoice-retry-export-policy.md`.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Do not change retry execution, customer notification, dunning workflow, or any
  behavior outside CSV row eligibility and CSV columns.
- Do not reintroduce retired `delinquent` status semantics.
- Do not apply enterprise status as an eligibility filter.
- Do not resume or derive authority from cancelled or done historical
  workstreams except as stale-history context.
- Do not add dependencies or replace the existing node test harness.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `exportRetryInvoicesCsv` exports rows where `status === "overdue"`,
  `retryEligible === true`, `accountType === "production"`, and
  `cancelledAt == null`.
- Production overdue retry-eligible enterprise invoices are exported.
- Production overdue retry-eligible non-enterprise invoices are exported.
- Test invoices are excluded.
- Cancelled invoices are excluded.
- Non-overdue invoices are excluded, including rows still using the retired
  `delinquent` status.
- Retry-ineligible invoices are excluded.
- The CSV header and column order remain exactly
  `invoice_id,account_id,amount_cents`.
- Source row order is preserved after filtering.
- The existing `npm test` script passes using the existing node test harness.

## Cold-Start Context

Governing records:

- `.10x/specs/invoice-retry-export.md` is the active behavioral contract for
  row eligibility and CSV columns.
- `.10x/decisions/invoice-retry-export-policy.md` rejects retired
  `delinquent`, enterprise-only filtering, and cancelled-invoice inclusion.

First source locations to inspect:

- `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.js` contains the export implementation.
- `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.test.js` contains the node test coverage.
- `package.json` defines `npm test` as
  `node --test src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.test.js`.

Historical records that are non-authoritative for current behavior:

- `.10x/tickets/done/2026-06-10-include-cancelled-invoice-retries.md` belongs
  to a superseded cancelled-invoice prototype.
- `.10x/tickets/cancelled/2026-06-12-enterprise-only-invoice-retry-export.md`
  was cancelled after Billing Ops confirmed the feed covers all production
  accounts.
- `.10x/evidence/2026-06-10-cancelled-invoice-prototype.md` records old V1
  evidence only and does not establish current behavior.

## Evidence Expectations

Before closure, record evidence showing:

- the relevant implementation diff aligns the filters with the active spec;
- node test coverage exercises each acceptance criterion above;
- `npm test` passes after the implementation change.

## Assumption Provenance

- `overdue` status, production account type, retry eligibility, cancellation
  exclusion, non-enterprise inclusion, exact CSV columns, source order
  preservation, no new dependencies, and node test harness usage are
  record-backed by `.10x/specs/invoice-retry-export.md`.
- Rejection of retired `delinquent`, enterprise-only filtering, and
  cancelled-invoice inclusion is record-backed by
  `.10x/decisions/invoice-retry-export-policy.md`.
- No semantic assumptions remain unresolved for this bounded implementation
  ticket.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from active spec, active decision, terminal ticket
  history, and read-only source inspection. Next concrete action is to update
  `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.js` and
  `src/invoices/exportRetryInvoices.test.js` within the scope above, then run
  `npm test` and record evidence.

## Blockers

None.
